commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.
target.author: stevenepalmer

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

target.title: fix(openai): avoid stale Responses message id replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Responses request replay can reuse a signed assistant `message` item id after the corresponding `reasoning` item has been pruned from local history.
- Solution: replay signed assistant message ids only when the immediately preceding replayed item is the paired reasoning item.
- What changed: added a small replay-id policy helper, wired it into the OpenAI Responses transport, and covered the pruned-reasoning case in the existing reasoning replay tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change reasoning-item replay, tool-call replay, payload metadata, model selection, or Telegram formatting.

## Motivation

This prevents a latent OpenAI Responses failure exposed by long-running sessions whose assistant text still has a persisted Responses item id but whose paired reasoning item is no longer replayable. In that state, the next request can be rejected before the model responds.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses rejected replayed conversation history when an assistant `message` item id was included without the required paired `reasoning` item.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 using the OpenAI Responses transport path for `openai/gpt-5.4`, plus OpenAI Node SDK 6.38.0 in-process Responses loopback against PR head `28cb12413a5950414db1d8ec2bdaae106d84202f`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts
env TMPDIR=/private/tmp node --import tsx .proof/openai-responses-sdk-loopback-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Before this patch, the live OpenClaw Telegram agent diagnosed a real OpenAI Responses failure after conversation pruning:

```text
OpenAI rejected the next request with:
message was provided without its required reasoning item

Failure shape:
- prior assistant message ids were replayed from textSignature
- paired reasoning items had been pruned/unavailable
```

After this patch, the targeted OpenClaw source test passed and verifies that the stale signed message id is omitted unless paired reasoning was replayed:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Volumes/beatrice/.openclaw/workspace/out/openclaw-upstream

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
   Start at  23:09:22
   Duration  29.32s (transform 1.37s, setup 0ms, import 19.41s, tests 3.44s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 241.16s
```

Additional after-fix real-SDK loopback proof from PR head `28cb12413a5950414db1d8ec2bdaae106d84202f` used the patched OpenClaw Responses payload builder and OpenAI Node SDK 6.38.0 with an in-process `fetch` loopback. The SDK serialized and sent the request to `/v1/responses`, the loopback returned a successful streamed Responses result, and the captured SDK request still omitted the stale signed assistant message id:

```shell
env TMPDIR=/private/tmp node --import tsx .proof/openai-responses-sdk-loopback-proof.ts
```

```json
{
  "proof": "openai-responses-sdk-loopback",
  "pr_head": "28cb12413a5950414db1d8ec2bdaae106d84202f",
  "request_path": "/v1/responses",
  "sdk_stream_completed": true,
  "sdk_events_seen": 2,
  "stale_signed_message_id": "msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning",
  "stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": false,
  "stale_id_present_in_sdk_request": false,
  "redacted_input_items": [
    {
      "role": "developer",
      "id": null,
      "phase": null
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "id": null,
      "phase": null
    },
    {
      "type": "message",
      "role": "assistant",
      "id": null,
      "phase": "final_answer"
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "id": null,
      "phase": null
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: signed assistant message ids are no longer replayed when their required reasoning item is absent; signed ids are still replayed when the immediately preceding replayed item is reasoning, and unsigned assistant messages still get the existing synthetic fallback id.
- What was not tested: a live deployed gateway restart or live OpenAI API request with this exact source branch has not been run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live OpenClaw agent output above captured the OpenAI rejection text and identified the pruned-reasoning replay shape.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the Responses replay path treated persisted assistant text signatures as independently replayable message ids, but OpenAI can require the corresponding reasoning item to be replayed with that message item.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage checked reasoning replay when reasoning was present; it did not check the pruned-reasoning case where only the signed assistant message survived.
- Contributing context (if known): long-lived sessions can prune or omit reasoning items while retaining assistant text signatures.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a signed assistant message id is omitted when no paired reasoning item was replayed; the same signed id is preserved when paired reasoning was replayed; unsigned text still uses the fallback id.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in request payload construction, before network IO, so the replay-id policy can be tested deterministically.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for the pruned-reasoning signed-message case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None, except affected OpenAI Responses sessions avoid a provider-side 400 when replay history has a stale signed message id without its paired reasoning item.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request replays message id] -> [OpenAI rejects]

After:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request omits stale message id] -> [request remains valid]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS arm64
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest via OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.4`, OpenAI Responses
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram agent session exposed the failure; source regression covers the transport replay policy.
- Relevant config (redacted): OpenAI Responses API model with reasoning enabled.

### Steps

1. Create a replay candidate with a signed assistant text `message` id.
2. Omit the paired reasoning item, matching a pruned history state.
3. Build the next OpenAI Responses request.

### Expected

- The stale signed assistant message id is omitted when paired reasoning is unavailable.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale signed assistant message id was replayed even when the paired reasoning item was unavailable.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted replay-policy regression via `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts`; real-SDK in-process Responses loopback via `env TMPDIR=/private/tmp node --import tsx .proof/openai-responses-sdk-loopback-proof.ts`; whitespace validation via `git diff --check`.
- Edge cases checked: signed id without reasoning is omitted; signed id with reasoning is preserved; unsigned assistant text keeps the synthetic fallback id.
- What you did **not** verify: live gateway deployment on this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: omitting a stale Responses message id may reduce provider-side continuity for that one historical assistant message.
  - Mitigation: the text content and phase metadata are still replayed; the id is omitted only when the paired reasoning item is missing, which is the provider-rejected shape.
target.author: latensified
